title: Seafood Chowder
description: A beautiful seafood chowder brimming with mixed seafood, tender potato, carrot, and sweet corn in a creamy soup broth infused with seafood flavour
author_notes: Recipe designed to be easy using store-bought seafood marinara mix and chicken stock rather than fish stock. Fish sauce adds umami depth. Bacon and butter enhance flavour.

ingredients.item: seafood marinara mix or mixed fresh seafood
ingredients.original_text: 650g/ 1.3lb seafood marinara mix or mixed fresh seafood – fish, squid, prawns, cooked mussel meat
1/2 cup chardonnay or other dry white wine (can skip)
1 litre (4 cups) chicken stock/broth, low sodium OR homemade fish stock
2 medium carrots, peeled, cut into 7mm pieces
2 large potatoes, peeled, cut into 1cm cubes
1 cup thickened cream / heavy cream (sub milk for lighter option)
3 tbsp chives or parsley, finely chopped, for garnish

steps.instruction: Separate raw and cooked seafood from marinara mix. Cut large fish pieces into 2.5cm cubes.
Cook bacon in butter until light golden, then remove with slotted spoon, leaving butter and bacon fat in pot.
Sauté garlic briefly, then deglaze with white wine on high heat for 3 minutes, scraping base to loosen bacon bits, until wine mostly evaporates.
Reduce heat to medium, add flour and mix for 1 minute to form roux.
Slowly pour in 1 cup stock while stirring vigorously until roux dissolves. Add remaining stock and whisk until lump-free.
Increase heat to high, bring to boil, add carrots, potatoes and bacon. Lower heat to medium and simmer 10-12 minutes until carrots and potatoes are almost done.
Add cream, fish sauce, pepper, corn and raw seafood. Simmer for exactly 3 minutes.
Stir in cooked seafood. Taste and adjust salt if needed.
Ladle into bowls and top with chopped chives or parsley. Serve with crusty bread for dunking.

title: Baked Sausage Breakfast Hash
description: A hearty breakfast hash loaded with sausages, bacon, eggs and potatoes, all baked in the oven for easy preparation.
author_notes: Bake up to just before cracking eggs; cool, cover, refrigerate overnight. Reheat at 180°C (160°C fan) for 10 minutes, then add eggs. Leftovers keep 3 days refrigerated or 3 months frozen (avoid freezing cooked eggs).

ingredients.original_text: 1/2 tsp dried thyme (sub any dried herb)
1 red capsicum / bell pepper, cut into 2.5cm squares
Eggs – as many as you want!

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F (180°C fan-forced). Toss potatoes with oil and seasoning; spread on lined tray in snug layer. Bake 15 minutes.
Toss capsicum and onion with oil, salt, pepper; add to tray and mix with potatoes.
Squeeze sausage meat from casings and dot over top; scatter with bacon. Bake 35 minutes total, tossing at 20-minute mark.
Lower oven to 180°C/350°F (160°C fan). Push potatoes aside to create wells; crack eggs into wells.
Bake 7-10 minutes until eggs reach desired doneness (~8 minutes for runny, 10 for fully cooked).
Scatter with parsley and serve immediately.

title: Marinated Fish Tacos
description: Fish marinated in chipotle lime with fresh Mexican flavours, seared until golden and served in tortillas with pickled cabbage and sriracha sour cream sauce.
author_notes: Favourite fish: snapper and barramundi for meaty texture. Avoid lean fish (swordfish, tuna) and delicate fish (sole, flounder). Marinate maximum 20 minutes to prevent lime juice from overcooking fish.

ingredients.original_text: 3 green onion stems, finely sliced on the diagonal

steps.instruction: Combine fish marinade ingredients in a ziplock bag. Set aside for 20 minutes to marinate – no longer than 1 hour.
Place pickled cabbage ingredients in a bowl. Toss to combine and set aside for 30 minutes. Drain excess liquid and scrunch cabbage with hands to soften.
Mix sour cream and sriracha together to create pink taco sauce.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook fish for 2 minutes on each side until golden and cooked through.
Remove fish to plate and flake into large pieces.
Assemble tacos by topping tortillas with pickled cabbage, flaked fish, dollop of pink sauce, and lime juice squeeze. Fold and serve.

title: Carrot Cake
description: Easy and ultra moist carrot cake with cream cheese frosting, featuring cinnamon, nuts, coconut, and crushed pineapple for moisture and flavour.
author_notes: Use block cream cheese, not spreadable. Beat frosting 3-5 minutes for fluffiness. Flip cakes upside down before frosting for level surface. Don't bake in single round pan - too dense.

ingredients.original_text: 440g / 20 oz can crushed pineapple, drained but RESERVE juice
1/4 cup (65 ml) reserved canned pineapple juice
3/4 cup (185 ml) milk, at room temperature
2 cups grated carrot, about 2 carrots, peeled
1/4 cup coconut, shredded or desiccated (plain / unsweetened)
1/2 cup walnuts or pecans, roughly chopped
4 cups (480g) icing sugar / powdered sugar, sifted if clumpy

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F. Grease and line two 20-22.5cm round pans or 9x13" rectangle pan with parchment paper.
Drain crushed pineapple well, reserving 1/4 cup of juice.
Whisk dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon) in a large bowl.
In separate bowl, whisk together milk, vinegar, eggs, brown sugar, oil and 1/4 cup pineapple juice.
Stir carrot, crushed pineapple, coconut and pecans into wet ingredients.
Pour wet into dry ingredients, stir until flour is incorporated.
Pour into prepared pans. Bake round cakes 35 minutes, rectangle 40 minutes, until skewer comes out clean.
Rest 10 minutes in pan before turning out onto cooling rack. Frost when completely cool.
For frosting: Beat cream cheese, butter and vanilla until smooth. Beat in icing sugar in 2 batches until fluffy, about 3 minutes.
Flip one cake upside down on serving platter. Spread with 1/3 frosting. Place second cake upside down on frosting. Frost top and sides with remaining frosting.

title: Pineapple Fried Rice (Thai)
description: Thai pineapple fried rice with jasmine rice, fresh or canned pineapple, and vegetables in a savoury-sweet sauce. Quick side dish with tropical flair.
author_notes: Use day-old jasmine rice for best results. Fried rice needs dried-out rice to avoid a gluey mess. Freezing cooked rice for 1 hour then thawing creates ideal texture.

ingredients.original_text: 3 cups day old jasmine rice, cooked
1 1/2 cups pineapple pieces, fresh or canned drained

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a wok or large non-stick skillet over high heat.
Add garlic and onion, cook for 1 minute.
Add capsicum, cook for 1 minute.
Add peas, stir for 30 seconds.
Add rice and sauce ingredients. Cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes until rice caramelises.
Add pineapple, stir for 30 seconds to warm through.
Stir through green onions and serve.

title: Bok Choy in Ginger Sauce
description: Quick Asian greens dish with ginger-infused sauce, ready in 5 minutes. Pan-roasted and steamed bok choy coated in a shiny ginger sauce.
author_notes: Works with other Asian greens like pak choy and choy sum. Sauce relies on water from steaming vegetables. Can substitute oyster sauce with vegetarian version or hoisin.

ingredients.original_text: 6 small bok choys, up to ~17cm long, or other Asian greens
1/4 cup ginger, finely julienned (or 1 tbsp garlic)
1 1/2 tsp light soy sauce, or all-purpose soy

steps.instruction: Trim base of bok choy, separate leaves, keep delicate center intact. Cut large stems in half lengthwise. Rinse in colander, shake off excess water.
Mix sauce ingredients except water in a jug until cornflour dissolves. Stir in water.
Place ginger and oil in large non-stick pan on medium heat. Sauté for 1 minute until golden. Add bok choy and toss for 15 seconds to coat in gingery oil.
Increase heat to medium, pour water over greens. Cover with lid and steam for 45 seconds until stem is partially cooked but still has soft crunch.
Remove lid, stir sauce quickly, pour over bok choy. Toss for 30 seconds until sauce becomes clear and glossy, thickening slightly.
Transfer bok choy and all sauce to serving plate. Serve immediately, preferably over rice or noodles.

title: Korean Beef Bulgogi Rice Bowls (Easy)
description: An easy version of Korean Beef Bulgogi made with ground beef rather than pricey steaks but using an authentic Bulgogi marinade. Serve as rice bowls, noodles, wraps, burritos or tacos.
author_notes: Don't skip the apple and onion - grated onion adds flavour in a way diced never will, and apple adds sweetness and tenderises. Mirin adds depth but is optional.

ingredients.original_text: 500g beef mince (ground beef), any fat % you like
1 tbsp canola oil + 2 tsp (to wilt baby spinach)
3 tbsp red apple, finely grated (skin on is fine, ~1/2 apple)
2 1/2 tbsp soy sauce – all-purpose or light
1 tbsp mirin, omit for no alcohol
2 tsp rice vinegar (or apple cider vinegar)
1 large carrot, peeled and julienned or grated
1 1/2 cups baby cos / romaine lettuce, torn

steps.instruction: Mix the Bulgogi sauce in a bowl, and the rice bowl drizzle sauce in a separate bowl.
Heat 2 teaspoons of oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat. Add half the spinach, toss until semi wilted (30 sec), then add the rest. Toss until wilted (1 min) then remove.
Add 1 tbsp oil to pan. Still on high heat, add beef and cook, breaking up, until no longer raw.
Add Bulgogi sauce and simmer rapidly, stirring occasionally, until sauce is mostly evaporated.
Stir regularly until beef gets caramelisation but is still moist.
Spoon beef over rice, place spinach and carrot. Top with pickled ginger and avocado. Tuck lettuce down the side. Sprinkle sesame seeds and green onion. Drizzle sauce.

title: Chicken Chasseur
description: A classic French dish of chicken pieces cooked in a rich mushroom and tomato sauce laced with brandy, finished with fresh tarragon.
author_notes: JB's debut recipe – learned in cooking school and tweaked over years. Serve with mashed potato.

ingredients.original_text: 4 chicken thighs, bone-in, skin on (~250g each)
2 onions, brown or yellow, halved and sliced 6mm thick
1/2 cup chardonnay or other dry white wine

steps.instruction: Pat chicken dry. Season with salt and pepper. Coat in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high. Add butter. Place thighs skin-side down, sear 5 min until golden. Flip, cook 1 min. Transfer to plate. Brown drumsticks on 3 sides, ~2 min per side.
In same pan, increase heat to high. Add mushrooms and onions. Cook 5 min, stirring. Add garlic and cook 30 sec.
Pour in brandy. Flambé if desired, or bubble 20-30 sec. Add wine and reduce by half, scraping fond. Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 min. Add stock, salt, pepper.
Return chicken skin-side up, nestle into sauce. Bring to simmer. Reduce heat to medium-low. Cover and simmer 10 min.
Remove lid and simmer uncovered for 20 min to reduce sauce.
Transfer chicken to plate. Turn heat to medium-high and reduce sauce 3 min. Off heat, stir in cold butter cubes until glossy.
Return chicken to sauce. Sprinkle with tarragon and serve with mash.

title: Filipino Pork Adobo
description: Pork braised until meltingly tender in an intense soy-garlic sauce that reduces to a glaze, served over steaming rice.
author_notes: Pork shoulder preferred over belly for less fat. Use pork parts ribboned with fat. Slow cooker not recommended. Pineapple optional but recommended.

ingredients.original_text: 1 kg pork shoulder (parts ribboned with fat), skinless, cut into 6cm cubes
1 large onion, halved then sliced into wedges
6 pineapple rings, each cut into 8 pieces (canned in juice)

steps.instruction: Heat oil in large heavy pot over high heat. Sear half the pork until golden all over (~4 min), remove. Repeat with remaining pork.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion and cook 2 min. Add garlic and cook 1 min.
Add water, soy sauces, vinegar, sugar, peppercorns and bay leaves. Return pork.
Bring to boil, partially cover (2cm crack), reduce to low. Simmer 1.5 hr until pork is fall-apart tender.
Meanwhile heat 2 tsp oil, cook pineapple golden each side (~4 min/side). Set aside.
Remove pork with slotted spoon. Boil sauce 7-10 min to reduce to 1.5 cups.
Reduce heat to low. Return pork and pineapple, stir gently to coat and warm.
Serve over jasmine rice, garnished with green onion.

title: Vietnamese Caramel Pork
description: Tender pork shoulder braised in a sweet savoury glaze made with coconut water, brown sugar, fish sauce and garlic. Also known as Thit Kho To.
author_notes: Use pork shoulder or belly cut into 3cm pieces. Coconut water is essential. Don't use tender cuts like loin. If pork isn't tender when liquid reduces, add 0.5 cup water and continue.

ingredients.original_text: 1 kg pork shoulder or boneless skinless pork belly, cut into 3 cm pieces
Red chilli and finely sliced shallots/green onions, to garnish

steps.instruction: Place brown sugar and water in a large pot over medium heat. Stir until sugar melts and caramelises. Add pork, coconut water, shallot, garlic, fish sauce and white pepper.
Stir and adjust heat to a fairly energetic simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 1.5 hours, stirring once or twice.
When liquid has reduced and pork is tender, the fat will separate. Stir and the pork will brown and caramelise in the fat.
Once liquid is gone and glaze coats the pork, it's ready.
Serve over rice, garnished with fresh chilli and shallots.
